name: Fetch Target Sequence
description: Resolves a gene name, local resource name, or raw DNA sequence into a clean DNA string ready for guide design. Resolution order: (1) raw DNA string — returned as-is; (2) local MCP resource name (e.g. 'ecoli_rpsl', 'pBR322') — read from bundled file; (3) gene name + organism — fetched live from NCBI Entrez. Use this tool at the start of any workflow where you have a gene name but not yet a sequence.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: A raw DNA sequence (A/T/G/C only), a local resource name (e.g. 'ecoli_rpsl', 'pBR322'), or a gene name (e.g. 'rpsL', 'lacZ', 'recA'). The tool resolves whichever form is provided.

inputs.name: organism
inputs.description: Organism for NCBI gene search. Only used when query is a gene name. Default: 'Escherichia coli'. Examples: 'Homo sapiens', 'Mus musculus', 'Danio rerio'.

outputs.description: A dictionary with: sequence (clean uppercase DNA string), source ('raw_input' | 'local_resource' | 'ncbi'), resource (resource key or gene name used), organism (organism queried), length (int, bp), note (provenance note). NCBI results also include ncbi_gene_id and ncbi_accession.
